raw.title: Rethinking our place in nature means rethinking the law
raw.excerpt: As Indigenous knowledge gains recognition and environmental crises deepen, a growing movement argues that granting legal rights to nature can protect it from exploitation.

llm_processed.summary: This piece explores rethinking legal systems to recognize nature's intrinsic value, drawing on Indigenous traditions of coexistence and challenging anthropocentric legal frameworks.
llm_processed.lateral_potential: Links Indigenous ecological wisdom to commons governance, enabling legal innovations for ecological restoration.
